description: Stock analysis dashboard — technical indicators, fundamental scores, and market data from yfinance → InfluxDB

panels.description: Chat-style financial Q&A. Ask natural-language questions about stock data in InfluxDB — the AI generates Flux queries, executes them, and formats the answer.

options.prompt:
You are an expert data analyst reviewing Grafana panel data.

Given the panel configuration and query results below, provide:
1. A summary of what metrics/data are being displayed
2. Key trends or patterns in the data
3. Any anomalies or notable observations
4. Actionable insights or recommendations

Be concise but thorough. Use bullet points for clarity.

panels.title: Ask AI

panels.description: Business summary, industry classification, and last-updated timestamp for each tracked stock.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "company_info")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "ticker", "industry"])

panels.title: Company Details

panels.description: Latest trade price from [yfinance](https://pypi.org/project/yfinance/). Updated each export cycle.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Current Price

panels.description: Highest trade price in the past 52 weeks. Proximity may indicate [resistance](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/resistance.asp) or a breakout.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_high")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: 52-Week High

panels.description: Lowest trade price in the past 52 weeks. Proximity may indicate [support](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/support.asp) or further downside.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_low")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: 52-Week Low

panels.description: [Market capitalization](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/marketcapitalization.asp) = share price × shares outstanding. Micro (<$300M), Small (<$2B), Mid (<$10B), Large (<$200B), Mega (>$200B).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "market_cap")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Market Cap

panels.description: [Price-to-Earnings ratio](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/price-earningsratio.asp) (trailing 12 months). Lower values may indicate undervaluation relative to earnings; high values suggest growth expectations or overvaluation. Negative = unprofitable.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "pe_ratio")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: P/E Ratio

panels.description: [Piotroski F-Score](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/piotroski-score.asp) (0–9). Measures financial strength across profitability, leverage, liquidity, and operating efficiency. ≥7 = strong fundamentals; ≤3 = weakness.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "piotroski_score")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Piotroski Score

panels.description: [Bollinger Bands](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/bollingerbands.asp) signal (Buy / Hold / Sell). Buy = price near lower band (potential [support](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/support.asp)); Sell = price near upper band (potential [resistance](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/resistance.asp)).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r.bollinger_signal, ticker: r.ticker}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Bollinger Signal

panels.description: [Implied volatility](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/iv.asp) (IV) from nearest-expiry ATM call option. Higher IV = market expects larger price swings. Useful for [options](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/option.asp) pricing and gauging uncertainty.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "iv")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Implied Volatility

panels.description: [Bid-ask spread](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/bid-askspread.asp) as a percentage of the midpoint price. Lower = more liquid; wider spreads indicate lower liquidity or higher volatility. Useful for assessing [market depth](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/marketdepth.asp) and trading costs.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "bid_ask_spread_pct")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Bid/Ask Spread %

panels.description: Calendar days until the next [earnings date](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/earningsreport.asp) from yfinance. Approaching dates (<7 days) are highlighted in red.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "days_until_earnings")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Days Until Earnings

panels.description: Consensus [EPS](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/eps.asp) estimate for the upcoming quarter from analyst forecasts via yfinance. Positive = profitable expectations; negative = expected loss.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "earnings_estimate_avg")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: EPS Estimate (Avg)

panels.description: Consensus [revenue](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/revenue.asp) estimate for the upcoming quarter from analyst forecasts via yfinance.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "revenue_estimate_avg")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Revenue Estimate

panels.description: 1-minute [OHLCV](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/ohlcchart.asp) close prices for the most recent trading session(s).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_intraday")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "close")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: r.ticker}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: Intraday Price (1-min)

panels.description: Uses the Price History Range dropdown instead of the dashboard time picker

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -${price_range})
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "close")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: r.ticker}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: Price History — ${ticker} (${price_range})

panels.description: Daily close overlaid with [Simple Moving Averages](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/sma.asp) (50, 100, 150, 200-day). Price crossing above/below an MA may signal trend changes. The 200-day MA is a widely-watched long-term trend indicator.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "close" or r["_field"] == "ma_50" or r["_field"] == "ma_100" or r["_field"] == "ma_150" or r["_field"] == "ma_200")

panels.title: Price & Moving Averages

panels.description: [Relative Strength Index](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/rsi.asp) (14-period). Oscillator 0–100. >70 = [overbought](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/overbought.asp) (potential pullback); <30 = [oversold](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/oversold.asp) (potential bounce). Shaded zones highlight these regions.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "rsi")

panels.title: RSI (14)

panels.description: [Moving Average Convergence Divergence](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/macd.asp). MACD crossing above the signal line = bullish; below = bearish. Histogram bars show the gap — growing bars = strengthening momentum.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd" or r["_field"] == "macd_signal")

from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd_histogram")

panels.title: MACD

panels.description: AI-powered analysis of [RSI](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/rsi.asp) data. LLM identifies trends, overbought/oversold conditions, and actionable insights.

options.prompt:
You are an expert financial analyst with a data science background reviewing RSI data for the given stock.

Given the panel configuration and query results below, provide:
1. A summary of what metrics/data are being displayed
2. Key trends or patterns in the data
3. Any anomalies or notable observations
4. Actionable market insights and/or recommendations

Be concise but thorough. Use bullet points for clarity.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "rsi")

panels.title: RSI - AI Analysis

panels.description: AI-powered analysis of [MACD](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/macd.asp) data. LLM interprets signal crossovers, histogram momentum, and trend direction.

options.prompt:
You are an expert financial analyst with a data science background reviewing MACD data for the given stock. In the query, you will see MACD Signal and Histogram data points, as well as the stock's MACD.

Given the panel configuration and query results, provide:
1. A summary of what metrics/data are being displayed
2. Key trends or patterns in the data
3. Any anomalies or notable observations
4. Actionable market insights and/or recommendations

Be concise but thorough. Use bullet points for clarity.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd" or r["_field"] == "macd_signal")

from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd_histogram")

panels.title: MACD AI Analysis

panels.description: [Volume Rate of Change](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/v/volumepricetrend.asp) — % change in volume over a lookback period. Spikes may confirm price moves (high conviction) or signal reversals.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "vroc")

panels.title: VROC

panels.description: [Implied volatility](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/iv.asp) overlaid with current price. Rising IV with falling price may signal fear; rising IV with rising price may indicate speculative momentum.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price" or r["_field"] == "iv")

panels.title: IV vs Price

panels.description: Earnings announcements can affect a stock's price. This table shows the stock's price the day before and the day after recent earnings reports, including the percentage change.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -5y)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "earnings_price_change")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "price_before", "price_after", "pct_change"])
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"], desc: true)

panels.title: Earnings-Related Price Changes

panels.description:
Entry-point screener using the RSI + MACD crossover methodology.

**Buy Signal** fires when:
1. RSI is oversold (< 30)
2. MACD has crossed above its signal line (bullish crossover)

The 52-week low column provides historical support context.

targets.query:
import "join"

rsi_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "rsi")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "rsi"})
  |> group()

macd_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "macd"})
  |> group()

signal_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd_signal")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "macd_signal"})
  |> group()

price_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "current_price"})
  |> group()

low_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["ticker"] =~ /^${ticker:regex}$/)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_low")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "week_52_low"})
  |> group()

j1 = join.inner(left: rsi_data, right: macd_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: r.macd}))
j2 = join.inner(left: j1, right: signal_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: r.macd_signal}))
j3 = join.inner(left: j2, right: price_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: r.current_price}))
join.inner(left: j3, right: low_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({
  ticker: l.ticker,
  rsi: l.rsi,
  rsi_oversold: if l.rsi < 30.0 then 1 else 0,
  macd: l.macd,
  macd_signal: l.macd_signal,
  macd_bullish: if l.macd > l.macd_signal then 1 else 0,
  buy_signal: if l.rsi < 30.0 and l.macd > l.macd_signal then 1 else 0,
  current_price: l.current_price,
  week_52_low: r.week_52_low,
  pct_above_52low: (l.current_price - r.week_52_low) / r.week_52_low * 100.0
}))

panels.title: Entry Point Screener (RSI + MACD Crossover)

title: Stock Analysis Dashboard
